Juan Gomez

Position: Framer  ·  Supervisor: Freddy Davila

1
Incident Information
Employee NameJuan Gomez
PositionFramer

Date/Time of Medical Visit06-17-2026, 8:00 AM (arrival) – 9:30 AM (discharge)

2
Description of Incident

On 06-16-2026, at approximately 4:00 PM, Juan Gomez was cutting wood at the MISD Career and Technical Education Center job site when wood dust was thrown into his right eye. Juan was wearing his safety glasses at the time of the incident. Following the incident, Juan reported irritation in his right eye and was sent for medical evaluation.

3
Medical Treatment and Diagnosis

Juan was evaluated at Occucare International — Deer Park (321 W. San Augustine, Deer Park, TX 77536) on 06-17-2026 by D.P. Gonzalez, MD, and Cynthia Solis, PA. A fluorescein dye exam was performed on the right eye to check for corneal damage. The exam confirmed a scratch on the cornea; no foreign body was found in the eye.

Diagnosis
Abrasion of right cornea, initial encounter (ICD-10: S05.01XA)

Juan was prescribed eye drops to be used during the day and a gel to be used at night to reduce inflammation and irritation in the eye. A post-accident 10-panel drug screen and breath alcohol test were also administered on 06-17-2026 — both returned negative results.

Follow-up appointment: 06-18-2026 at 8:00 AM with the treating doctor.

4
Work Status
✓
Returned to full duty, no restrictions — effective 06-23-2026 (final release) Final visit: 06-23-2026  ·  Discharge: 9:00 AM  ·  No further follow-ups required  ·  Per DWC-073 Work Status Report

Activity restrictions: None.

5
Medical Evaluation Photos
6
Corrective Actions
  • Reinforce with all framing crew members that approved safety glasses must be worn at all times during wood-cutting operations.
  • Evaluate the use of safety glasses with side shields, or goggles, for tasks that generate airborne wood dust and debris.
  • Conduct a toolbox talk on eye protection from particulate hazards, using this incident as a case example.
  • Ensure Juan attends his scheduled follow-up appointment on 06-18-2026 and reports any change in symptoms to his supervisor immediately.
7
Follow-Up Appointment — 06-18-2026

Juan attended his first follow-up appointment on 06-18-2026 at 8:00 AM at Occucare International — Deer Park. The treating provider examined the right eye and noted that some irritation and redness were still present. Proparacaine Hydrochloride Ophthalmic Solution 0.5% (Sandoz) was administered to numb the eye and relieve pain during the examination. Juan was instructed to wear dark-tinted safety glasses to protect the eye from sunlight, and an eye patch was applied over the right eye to block light and prevent him from inadvertently touching it. A second follow-up appointment was scheduled for 06-23-2026.

8
Final Follow-Up Appointment — 06-23-2026

Juan attended his final follow-up appointment on 06-23-2026 at Occucare International — Deer Park. The treating provider (Cynthia Solis, PA — License #PA03187) examined the right eye. The eye had healed satisfactorily and Juan was cleared for full duty with no restrictions, effective 06-23-2026. Discharge time: 9:00 AM.

Final Diagnosis
Abrasion of right cornea, subsequent encounter (ICD-10: S05.01XD)

This was confirmed as the last scheduled visit for this injury. No further follow-up appointments or medical care are anticipated. Juan has been fully released to return to work without restrictions.
